name: Members Preferred
description_short: Members 1st Federal Credit Union is a Pennsylvania-based credit union offering checking, savings, certificates, loans, and credit cards with member-focused financial services.
description_long:
Members 1st Federal Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial institution based in Pennsylvania, serving individuals and families seeking alternatives to traditional banking. The organization emphasizes a mission-driven approach where member interests take priority over profit maximization, reflected in their tagline "Members 1st is more than just a name—it's a mindset."

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including 1st Defense checking accounts with built-in identity protection and rewards, high-yield savings accounts and certificates (currently offering 3.80% APY on 11-month certificates), auto loans, student loan alternatives, home mortgage services, and a Visa Signature credit card with 1.5% cash back and no annual fee. They also facilitate insurance products through third-party partners covering auto, health, life, and personal protection. Digital banking tools include goal-based savings accounts and a referral program offering $50 Amazon eGift cards.

What distinguishes Members 1st is their explicit focus on life milestones and community impact rather than purely transactional banking. They offer specialized guidance for major life events (homebuying, college savings, auto purchases) and promote fraud prevention through education. Their product features like identity protection bundled into checking accounts and travel/leisure discounts differentiate them from standard credit union offerings. The institution actively markets competitive savings rates and certificates to attract deposits.

As a credit union, Members 1st operates under different regulatory frameworks than banks, potentially offering more favorable rates and lower fees to members. However, membership eligibility requirements and geographic limitations to Pennsylvania service areas may restrict access. The website emphasizes promotional benefits but doesn't detail account minimums, fee structures, or membership eligibility criteria. Like most credit unions, they likely have smaller branch networks than major banks.
services: 1st Defense Checking accounts with identity protection and rewards
Certificates of Deposit (currently 3.80% APY on 11-month terms)
Student loan alternatives and college savings planning
Home mortgage services and homebuying guidance
Visa Signature credit card with 1.5% cash back
pros: 1st Defense checking includes bundled identity protection and travel/leisure discounts—differentiating feature absent from standard checking
Visa Signature card offers 1.5% cash back with no annual fee plus emergency medical coverage and trip interruption insurance
Competitive savings rates and certificates (11-month CD at 3.80% APY) actively promoted and recently increased
Goal-based savings tools with in-app tracking specifically designed for long-term financial planning
Member referral program providing $50 incentives for both referrer and new member
Comprehensive loan products including auto, mortgage, and student loan alternatives reducing reliance on federal student loans
Third-party insurance partnerships offering full range of personal, auto, health, and life coverage options
cons: Website does not disclose membership eligibility requirements or geographic service limitations beyond Pennsylvania reference
No transparent fee schedule or account minimum information provided on homepage or main service pages
Limited information about loan rates, terms, or approval criteria for auto, mortgage, or other loan products
As a credit union, likely smaller branch network and ATM availability compared to major national banks
No mention of mobile app features, online account opening process, or digital banking limitations
best_for: Pennsylvania residents seeking member-owned banking with emphasis on fraud protection and identity theft prevention
Individuals planning major life milestones (home purchase, college savings, auto loans) who value specialized financial guidance
Savers wanting competitive rates on certificates and savings accounts without sacrificing service quality
diagnosis: Members 1st Federal Credit Union is best for Pennsylvania residents prioritizing member-focused banking with bundled protections like identity theft coverage and competitive rates. The primary caveat is limited geographic availability (Pennsylvania-only) and lack of transparent fee/eligibility information on their website, requiring direct contact for specific account terms and membership requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
